items.title: AI coding at home without going broke
items.why_it_matters: A developer shared practical strategies for slashing AI coding costs by mixing high-end models with cheaper local or API alternatives without losing quality. This is highly relevant since you are vibe-coding and want to keep API costs low while building AI-ingest. This week, try configuring your coding setup to route routine tasks like writing CSS or basic tests to a cheaper fallback model (like DeepSeek or Mistral) to save your Claude quota for hard logic.

items.title: Show HN: Whim-proxy, a vibe-coded tool to reverse-tunnel webhooks to your laptop
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer vibe-coded a lightweight client-server tool to tunnel webhooks directly to a local development environment. Since you are building AI-ingest and managing GitHub commits, this makes local testing much easier. This week, clone the repo and use it to test a Supabase webhook locally on your laptop without having to deploy to Vercel first.

items.title: Claude Code v2.1.172: Sub-Agents Can Now Spawn Sub-Agents
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic updated Claude Code to allow sub-agents to spawn their own sub-agents, meaning the tool can now break down complex, multi-step coding tasks and delegate them automatically. This directly upgrades your primary coding tool. This week, run `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@latest` to update, then prompt it to build a complex feature for AI-ingest to watch how it orchestrates the sub-agents.

items.title: Show HN: I am running 3 coding agents non-stop over the last 3 days. Here is how
items.why_it_matters: A developer shared their setup for running Claude Code and other agents completely headless in the background using a task queue and a custom 'ask human' tool. This is a great blueprint for mastering the 'direction' part of vibe coding by letting the AI work quietly until it actually needs your input. This week, try running Claude Code with the headless `-p` flag on a simple refactoring task to see how it operates without constant prompting.

items.title: QodFlow – a Kanban board AI agents can drive via MCP
items.why_it_matters: QodFlow is a Kanban board designed specifically for AI agents to interact with directly via Model Context Protocol (MCP), allowing them to claim tasks, report progress, and request human decisions. Since you use Claude Code (which supports MCP), you can automate your project management. This week, sign up for a free QodFlow account, connect its MCP server to your Claude Code session, and ask Claude to update your task board as it writes code for AI-ingest.
